key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 formed a denser, more protective oxide layer than detonation-sprayed T-400, giving better resistance to hot corrosion in solar-salt exposure.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, the coating process strongly affects long-term durability in molten nitrate salts at 500 �b0C.
method: T-400 coatings were deposited on ASTM A516 steel using HVAF and detonation spraying.
Specimens were exposed to 60% NaNO3-40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 �b0C for up to 1000 h.
Microstructure, phase formation, and salt ingress were evaluated by SEM, elemental mapping, and phase analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr-Mo-Si-O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation over 1000 h.
watch_item: Results are specific to one alloy, one salt composition, and one temperature, so performance may differ under thermal cycling or other CSP operating conditions.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
